newsArray.title: Kohli, Rohit Set To Return; No Bumrah: India's Predicted Squad For AFG ODIs
newsArray.description: Virat Kohli and Rohit Sharma will make their return to international cricket in the three-match ODI series against Afghanistan. Both the former Indian captains are now only active in the ODI format. The selection committee could rest Jasprit Bumrah for the ODI series as he looks all but confirmed to play the one-off Test match. Here, we will have a look at India's predicted 15-member squad for the Afghanistan ODI series.
creator: rishav narang

newsArray.title: Brendon McCullum Drawn To Players Who Drink and Vape; Ben Stokes Blamed For Ashes 2025-26 Defeat: Report
newsArray.description: England's cricket leadership faces intense scrutiny as the England and Wales Cricket Board prepares to deliver its post-Ashes review. Discussions around team culture, player conduct, and coaching philosophy have intensified following the recent series defeat, with head coach Brendon McCullum defending his players amidst reports of off-field incidents.
creator: team latestly

newsArray.title: KKR face another injury blow as Akash Deep likely ruled out of IPL
newsArray.description: Kolkata Knight Riders (KKR) face another injury setback with pacer Akash Deep likely ruled out of the IPL season due to a lower-back stress injury. This adds to their bowling woes, with Harshit Rana and Matheesha Pathirana also unavailable.

newsArray.title: Irfan Pathan explains why MI has an edge over CSK in greatest IPL debate
newsArray.description: Irfan Pathan believes Mumbai Indians have an edge over Chennai Super Kings in the 'greatest IPL team' debate. He argues that despite equal titles, MI's significant contribution of nurturing match-winners for the Indian team sets them apart.
creator: asianet news central

newsArray.title: Pathan gives MI the edge in greatest IPL franchise debate ahead of CSK
newsArray.description: Both MI and CSK have won five IPL titles each, with tactical masterminds Rohit Sharma and MS Dhoni captaining them; there are not a lot of things separating them in the debate for the best IPL franchise. However, MI's scouting system and overall set-up have done wonders for both the franchise and the Indian cricket team, bringing in talents like Hardik Pandya, Jasprit Bumrah, Suryakumar Yadav, Ishan Kishan and Tilak Varma to Team India. These talents are just a few that have been discovered or backed by the Blue franchise, and this has increased India's ICC trophy cabinet in recent years.
creator: list.metadata.agency

newsArray.description: The Rs 25.20 deal by Kolkata Knight Riders in the IPL 2026 auction helped Cameron Green become the most expensive overseas player in IPL history.
